description:
You are working as an observability engineer who needs to do a very quick check of which services are currently defined in the local container stack before you start tuning the monitoring dashboards.

    1. A Compose file already exists at  
       /home/user/projects/metrics/docker-compose.yml

    2. Your goal is to produce a new log file named  
       /home/user/projects/metrics/defined_services.log

    3. Using a single shell command of your choice, extract the *service names* from the Compose file and write them—exactly in the order they appear in the file—to defined_services.log.  
       • One service name per line.  
       • No extra characters, spaces, or blank lines.  
       • A final newline after the last line is acceptable but not required.

    4. The automated checker will look for *precisely* the following two-line content inside /home/user/projects/metrics/defined_services.log:

       prometheus  
       grafana

    5. Do not create any extra files; simply generate the log at the exact path above with the exact content shown. The task can be completed with a single pipeline or compound command.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing file the agent will read -->
    <file path="/home/user/projects/metrics/docker-compose.yml"><![CDATA[
version: '3'
services:
  prometheus:
    image: prom/prometheus:latest
  grafana:
    image: grafana/grafana:latest
]]></file>

    <!-- File the agent must create -->
    <expected_file path="/home/user/projects/metrics/defined_services.log"><![CDATA[
prometheus
grafana
]]></expected_file>
name: task_000000_010b99da
